title: Those bhikkhus, having completed the rains retreat, after three months, started wandering. A devata dwelling in that forest, not seeing those bhikkhus, lamented and grieved,…
content:
Those bhikkhus, having completed the rains retreat, after three months, started wandering. A devata dwelling in that forest, not seeing those bhikkhus, lamented and grieved, and on that occasion spoke this verse:

Today my mind
Feels no joy,
Seeing many seats
Empty, without people.
Those learned ones,
Who taught the Dhamma so beautifully,
Disciples of Gotama,
Where are they now?"

Hearing this, another devata spoke these verses to that devata:

They have gone to Magadha,
They have gone to Kosala,
And some of them
Have gone to the Vajji country.
Like deer freed from a trap,
They run about in all directions.
Bhikkhus have no homes,
Thus they live liberated.

title: Friends, here there is a duty a bhikkhu has to do. He thinks: 'There is work I must do. If I do the work, my…
content: Friends, here there is a duty a bhikkhu has to do. He thinks: 'There is work I must do. If I do the work, my body will become tired. So I shall lie down.' He lies down, without striving to attain the unattained, to achieve the unachieved, to realize the unrealized. That is the first way of being slothful.

title: And, bhikkhus, what is a person with inverted wisdom? Here, bhikkhus, a person goes to the monastery and often hears the Dhamma from the bhikkhus.…
content: And, bhikkhus, what is a person with inverted wisdom? Here, bhikkhus, a person goes to the monastery and often hears the Dhamma from the bhikkhus. The bhikkhus teach him the Dhamma, beautiful in the beginning, beautiful in the middle, beautiful in the end, with meaning and phrasing, revealing the completely perfected and purified holy life. While sitting there, he does not attend to the beginning of that discourse, does not attend to the middle, does not attend to the end. Likewise, when he rises from that seat, he does not attend to the beginning, does not attend to the middle, does not attend to the end. Just as, bhikkhus, an overturned pot pours out the water contained in it and does not retain it, so too, bhikkhus, here a person goes to the monastery... does not attend to the end. Likewise, when he rises from that seat, he does not attend to the beginning... does not attend to the end. This, bhikkhus, is called a person with inverted wisdom.

title: 'What leads the world along? What torments oneself? Is there one thing To which everything is subject?' 'Craving leads the world along, Craving torments oneself,…
content:
'What leads the world along?
What torments oneself?
Is there one thing
To which everything is subject?'

'Craving leads the world along,
Craving torments oneself,
Craving is that one thing
To which everything is subject.'

title: Elder Kāḷudāyī: Blessed One, now is the time — the trees are blooming red, Having shed their old leaves, they seek the time of fruit.…
content:
Elder Kāḷudāyī:

Blessed One, now is the time — the trees are blooming red,
Having shed their old leaves, they seek the time of fruit.
They are like blazing fires, shining and radiant.
O great hero, now is the time to enjoy the taste.

The trees are blooming beautifully, spreading fragrance everywhere.
Having shed their leaves, longing for fruit, now is the time to depart,
To leave this place, O hero.

Neither too cold nor too hot — the weather is good, Blessed One.
Let the Sakyans and Koliyans see you,
Facing west, crossing the Rohiṇī river.

In hope, fields are plowed; in hope, seeds are sown.
In hope, merchants cross the sea to bring back wealth.
The hope that sustains me — may that hope succeed.

People continue to sow seeds, the sky continues to rain,
Farmers continue to plow, grain continues to come,
Coming abundantly to the country.

Beggars continue to beg, donors continue to give.
Donors who continue to give continue to go to heaven.

A hero continues — for up to seven generations
That family produces a person of vast wisdom.
I think you could be the lord of devas.
In you, a true seer has been born.

Your father, the great seer, is named King Suddhodana.
The mother of the Perfectly Enlightened One is named Māyā.
She, having conceived the Bodhisatta, passed away and attained heaven.

Gotamī, after death, from there, after passing away,
Enjoying fully the sensual pleasures of heaven,
She delights in the five strands of sense pleasure, surrounded by celestial nymphs.

I am the son of the Buddha who conquers the unconquered,
The Aṅgīrasa, the incomparable one.
The Sakyan king, my father's father,
The Gotama king is my grandfather — in truth it is so.

title: Bhikkhus, seeking what is wholesome, seeking the supreme, unsurpassed path of peace, I wandered by stages in the country of Magadha and arrived at the…
content: Bhikkhus, seeking what is wholesome, seeking the supreme, unsurpassed path of peace, I wandered by stages in the country of Magadha and arrived at the village of Uruvelā. There I saw a delightful place, a pleasant grove, a clear flowing river with a lovely ford, and a village nearby for alms. Then, bhikkhus, I thought: 'Truly, this is a delightful place, a pleasant grove, a clear flowing river with a lovely ford, and a village nearby for alms. This is suitable for a good man who wishes to strive.' And, bhikkhus, I sat down there, thinking: 'This is suitable for striving.'

title: Happy indeed is the person Who has nothing of one's own. One of perfect knowledge, Having nothing of one's own. Behold the affliction Of those…
content:
Happy indeed is the person
Who has nothing of one's own.
One of perfect knowledge,
Having nothing of one's own.

Behold the affliction
Of those who have possessions,
Which causes one person
To be bound to another.

title: - Whatever is dependent, that is agitated. Whatever is not dependent, that is not agitated. With no agitation, there is tranquility. With tranquility, there is…
content: - Whatever is dependent, that is agitated. Whatever is not dependent, that is not agitated. With no agitation, there is tranquility. With tranquility, there is no inclination. With no inclination, there is no coming and going. With no coming and going, there is no passing away and arising. With no passing away and arising, there is no this world, no next world, no intermediate state. This is the end of suffering.

title: Just as, brahmin, a blue lotus, or a red lotus, or a white lotus, born in the water, grown up in the water, rises up…
content: Just as, brahmin, a blue lotus, or a red lotus, or a white lotus, born in the water, grown up in the water, rises up above the water and stands without being stained by the water, so too, brahmin, I was born in the world, grown up in the world, yet having conquered the world, I dwell untainted by the world. Brahmin, remember me as the Buddha.

title: Friends, what is the development of concentration that, when developed and frequently practiced, leads to blissful abiding here and now? Here, friends, a bhikkhu, secluded…
content: Friends, what is the development of concentration that, when developed and frequently practiced, leads to blissful abiding here and now? Here, friends, a bhikkhu, secluded from sensual pleasures, secluded from unwholesome states, enters and abides in the first jhāna ... second jhāna ... third jhāna ... enters and abides in the fourth jhāna. Friends, this is the development of concentration that, when developed and frequently practiced, leads to blissful abiding here and now.
